text_blocks.snippet: Reunification counselling services in Vancouver and British Columbia (BC) are primarily structured to support families dealing with separation, divorce, high-conflict, or MCFD (Ministry of Children and Family Development) involvement. These services focus on rebuilding trust, improving communication, and facilitating safe, gradual, or, in some cases, intensive reconnection between children and parents. Reconnect Families +4
Key Organizations and Services in Vancouver/Lower Mainland

list.snippet: Family Services of Greater Vancouver (FSGV): Offers specialized Family Preservation and Reunification Services (often no-fee, with MCFD referral) that include in-home support, counseling for children/youth, and parenting education.

Reconnect Families (Vancouver/Surrey/Fraser Valley): Led by Bob Finlay, a registered clinical counsellor and family therapist. They specialize in parent-child reunification in cases of alienation, estrangement, or high-conflict, offering in-home, office, or intensive weekend sessions.

Bright Star Counselling (Port Moody): Provides a structured program led by Clinical Director Sonja Friedland, focusing on play-based sessions, individual parent/child sessions, and supported, supervised, or unsupervised,,, visitation.
Leanne Toews (Virtual/Specialized): Offers a 6-step,,, structured, often virtual, program designed for,, reconnection and co-parenting after,, separation, which can be adapted to be in-person.

Susan Gamache (REACH Program): Focuses on high-conflict, alienated, or estranged relationships, using a team approach with therapists for each family member to,, create,, better,, functioning,,, environments.
Touchstone Family Association: Provides MCFD-referred, in-home or office counseling to families in crisis, focusing on preventing family breakdown. Bright Star Counselling +7

MCFD-Referred (No-Fee): The Ministry of Children and Family Development (MCFD) funds several services, such as those provided by FSGV and Hollyburn Family Services, to support families where child protection concerns have been identified.

Private/Specialized Therapy: These programs, like Reconnect Families or Bright Star, are tailored for,, complex, cases and, are usually paid for,, by the, participants or through legal, fee arrangements.
In-Home Outreach: Programs offered by organizations like Touchstone and FSGV provide support directly in the home, often for 6–9 months, to help with daily routines and parenting skills.
Therapeutic Supervised Access: Services designed to facilitate safe, supervised,, contact while coaching parents, on, interaction techniques.
Intensive Weekends: Specialized, high-intensity, interventions, designed to, jumpstart, the, reconnection process. Bright Star Counselling +6
Client-Centered & Tailored: Services often begin with an assessment of the family's needs to create a customized, goal-oriented, plan.
Focus on the Child: The primary aim of,, most,, programs is to ensure the, well-being of the, child(ren).
Conflict Resolution & Communication: A core component of, counseling is teaching, families,, how, to, communicate, and, manage, conflict, effectively,. Bright Star Counselling +5

text_blocks.snippet: For immediate, no-fee services,,, referral, by, an, MCFD, social worker is, generally required. 211 British Columbia +1
